summary: This cluster focuses on the taxonomy and phylogenetic characterization of marine red algae, specifically within the genus *Neosiphonia* (Rhodophyta) from the Pacific temperate coast of South America. A key finding was the formal description of *Neosiphonia ramirezii* as a new species from Peru, distinguished by its unique prostrate system, ecorticate structure with four pericentral cells, and spirally arranged tetrasporangia. Molecular analysis using the *rbcL* gene marker confirmed its phylogenetic distinctness from closely related species such as *N. flaccidissima* and *N. savatieri*. The research underscores the utility of combining morphological traits with molecular phylogenetics to resolve species boundaries within this ecologically significant algal group.
